reviews.review_description:
Tank racing reminds me a lot of Mario Kart getting the crates and using items on the track. Here you use Tanks with multiple items to help you win the race. 

With two control options I see most using the more standard controls instead of the tank controls. Outside of that we had a "Blast" playing it.

What is Tank Racing? Imagine Mario Kart in VR — but with tanks! You’ll fight, push, blast, and outsmart your opponents to cross the finish line first. The game offers a great selection of power-ups like shields, mines, and boosters to keep each race exciting and unpredictable.

You can drive using the stick or take full advantage of the levers for an even more immersive tank experience. The pacing is fast, the action is intense, and the multiplayer mode adds a ton of replayability.

A fun and explosive racer that’s perfectly adapted to VR — chaotic in the best possible way!
Steering feels heavy at first, which can be tricky to learn. Switching to arcade steering in the garage makes it much easier and more fun, especially for new players.

This game is pure chaos in the best way. Tanks feel heavy and clunky at first, so I suggest switching to arcade mode — way easier to control. Tracks are fun, some are long, some are short and intense. Add weapons, mines, and the silly tank avatars, and it’s just a riot. Not super realistic, but honestly that’s what makes it so much fun.
The default steering takes some getting used to, but the arcade mode in the garage helps a lot. Once controls feel right, the races become fast, chaotic, and addictive.

The maps are really well made, with a good mix of long scenic tracks and short intense circuits. Every race feels different depending on the layout.
Gameplay feels like Mario Kart

There’s a nice variety of tanks to choose from, each with its own style. 
The tank avatars add a fun, playful vibe to the races. Combined with explosions and power-ups, it feels more like a chaotic VR party than a serious sim.
